text:
4. yes, I think the design should be, a message arrives to the inbox of a session, something drains it, either the pty-wrapper that injects as a user message, or the hook -- if they are atomic they can't be reinjected twice with no explicit dedup gate
5. yeah, only when older [...] Assistant: Critical finding: **the drain is _not_ atomic today.** `drain_pending_for_session` (inbox.rs:72) is a pure `SELECT`; marking happens separately via `mark_delivered` *after* rendering. So two paths can both select the same pending rows and double-deliver. To honor your #4, I'll convert it to an atomic claim (`UPDATE … SET state='delivered' … RETURNING`), so the first caller wins and the other gets nothing — atomicity *is* the dedup, no extra gate. (pty re-enqueues to `pending` if the paste itself fails, so a dead pane doesn't silently eat a message.)
